paper_metadata.title: Swin Transformer: Hierarchical Vision Transformer using Shifted Windows
paper_metadata.abstract: This paper presents a new vision Transformer, called Swin Transformer, that capably serves as a general-purpose backbone for computer vision. Challenges in adapting Transformer from language to vision arise from differences between the two domains, such as large variations in the scale of visual entities and the high resolution of pixels in images compared to words in text. To address these differences, we propose a hierarchical Transformer whose representation is computed with \textbf{S}hifted \textbf{win}dows. The shifted windowing scheme brings greater efficiency by limiting self-attention computation to non-overlapping local windows while also allowing for cross-window connection. This hierarchical architecture has the flexibility to model at various scales and has linear computational complexity with respect to image size. These qualities of Swin Transformer make it compatible with a broad range of vision tasks, including image classification (87.3 top-1 accuracy on ImageNet-1K) and dense prediction tasks such as object detection (58.7 box AP and 51.1 mask AP on COCO test-dev) and semantic segmentation (53.5 mIoU on ADE20K val). Its performance surpasses the previous state-of-the-art by a large margin of +2.7 box AP and +2.6 mask AP on COCO, and +3.2 mIoU on ADE20K, demonstrating the potential of Transformer-based models as vision backbones. The hierarchical design and the shifted window approach also prove beneficial for all-MLP architectures. The code and models are publicly available at~\url{https://github.com/microsoft/Swin-Transformer}.

extraction_data.innovation: Swin Transformer introduces a hierarchical vision Transformer with shifted windows, enabling linear computational complexity with respect to image size and efficient modeling of multi-scale visual features through local attention and window shifting.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Not explicitly stated in the provided text
extraction_data.application: Image classification, object detection, semantic segmentation
extraction_data.research_problem: Adapting Transformers from natural language to computer vision, particularly addressing challenges in scale variation and high-resolution image processing.
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Image classification, object detection, semantic segmentation
extraction_data.extension: The shifted window approach enables local self-attention computation within non-overlapping windows while allowing cross-window connections between consecutive layers, improving modeling capacity and efficiency.
extraction_data.paper_title: Swin Transformer: Hierarchical Vision Transformer using Shifted Windows

extraction_data.innovation: Introduces shifted window partitioning in successive Transformer blocks to enable cross-window connections while maintaining efficient, window-based self-attention computation. This allows modeling long-range dependencies in high-resolution images with quadratic complexity only within local windows.
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Image classification, object detection, semantic segmentation
extraction_data.extension: Shifted window mechanism enables local self-attention within non-overlapping windows and cross-window connections via shifting, improving efficiency and modeling capability.
extraction_data.research_problem: High-resolution image understanding with efficient, scalable self-attention in vision Transformers
extraction_data.application: Computer vision, image classification, object detection, instance segmentation, semantic segmentation
extraction_data.paper_title: Swin Transformer: Hierarchical Vision Transformer using Shifted Windows

extraction_data.innovation: Swin Transformer introduces a hierarchical vision transformer with shifted windows, enabling efficient computation and modeling of long-range dependencies in images. The shifted window mechanism allows for local self-attention within non-overlapping windows, while cross-window connections are established through shifting, improving both performance and scalability.
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Image classification, object detection, semantic segmentation
extraction_data.extension: Cyclic shift-based batch computation enables efficient handling of shifted windows without increasing the number of windows, maintaining computational efficiency.
extraction_data.research_problem: Image classification, object detection, instance segmentation, semantic segmentation
extraction_data.application: Computer vision, image classification, object detection, instance segmentation, semantic segmentation
